overall.description: Third-person gameplay footage from Assassin's Creed Mirage showing a hooded protagonist navigating a historic Middle Eastern city. The character moves through covered bazaar streets with fabric awnings, crosses an open plaza with a monumental tree, passes through a large stone archway, and continues through a bustling market district toward a riverside walkway.

scene_state.functional_zone: covered bazaar street with hanging fabric awnings

agents.ego_description: jogging through covered bazaar street beneath colorful red and green fabric awnings

entered.detail: large red fabric awning stretched across the street providing shade
green fabric canopy hanging between stone buildings
sandstone building with arched doorways and decorative stonework
projecting wooden lattice balcony on upper floor of building

segments.scene_description: The character moves through a covered bazaar street shaded by colorful fabric canopies. Sandstone buildings with arched doorways and wooden balconies line the path, while scattered pedestrians in robes walk through the warm sunlight filtering through the cloth.

scene_state.functional_zone: open plaza with monumental central tree

events.description: exits covered bazaar street into open sunlit plaza with large central tree

agents.ego_description: jogging across open plaza toward monumental stone archway in the distance

entered.detail: massive spreading tree providing shade in the center of the plaza
large ornate stone archway with steps leading up to it

segments.scene_description: Emerging from the covered bazaar, the scene opens into a sunlit plaza dominated by a massive central tree. The character jogs toward a monumental stone archway in the distance while pedestrians move through the open space around the tree.

scene_state.functional_zone: market district with wooden stalls and scaffolding

events.description: passes through monumental stone archway into bustling market district

agents.ego_description: passing through the stone archway and navigating between market stalls and vendors

notable_agents.action: vendor standing beside pottery stall watching the crowd

entered.detail: market stall with fabric canopy roof displaying goods
wooden market structure with shelves and barrels
stacked wooden barrels and sacks of goods

segments.scene_description: Passing through the monumental archway, the character enters a bustling market district with wooden stalls and construction scaffolding. Vendors tend to their goods while the character navigates between stalls displaying pottery and textiles on cobblestone ground.

events.description: reaches waterfront with river visible between buildings and palm trees

agents.ego_description: jogging along narrow riverside walkway beside the water, passing vendors and shoppers

entered.detail: calm river water visible between buildings and palm trees
wooden cart loaded with sacks and goods parked beside building
stone steps leading up to building entrance

segments.scene_description: The character emerges onto a narrow riverside walkway with the river visible to the left between palm trees. The path runs between the water and stone buildings, with wooden carts and market stalls lining the route as the character approaches a stone staircase.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement from the shaded intimacy of a covered bazaar into the expansive brightness of a city plaza and finally toward a riverside walkway. It begins beneath colorful fabric canopies and sandstone balconies where warm sunlight filters through the cloth, before emerging from the bazaar into a wide, sunlit plaza dominated by a massive central tree. The camera then follows the character through a monumental stone archway, shifting the environment into a bustling market district filled with wooden stalls, pottery, and construction scaffolding on cobblestone ground. Finally, the scene opens up as the character leaves the market to enter a narrow riverside walkway, moving past palm trees and wooden carts toward a stone staircase.
